message:
test(ffs-core): add btrfs zero-range allocation regression coverage (bd-qk5kv)

Two new ffs-core unit tests that lock the bd-qk5kv invariants the
preceding refactor (875623d, e166fbb, 86bfb46) was put together to
preserve. Plus a small whitespace cleanup on the prealloc-coalesce
helper introduced in e166fbb.

btrfs_write_fallocate_zero_range_preserves_regular_allocation
  - Creates a real btrfs file via FsOps::create / write, then
    issues a zero_range fallocate covering an existing Regular
    extent, and asserts that:
      * the resulting on-disk extent is still Regular (not silently
        turned into Prealloc),
      * the extent's logical layout is unchanged (no fragmentation
        from the rewrite),
      * the data range covered by zero_range reads back as zeros.
    This is the case the previous test pair did not cover — a
    Regular extent that gets zero-ranged in the middle.

btrfs_write_fallocate_zero_range_allocates_unwritten_hole
  - Creates a btrfs file, leaves a hole, then issues zero_range
    over the hole. Asserts:
      * a Prealloc extent is created (not nothing — zero_range over
        a hole must allocate space, that's the contract that
        distinguishes it from punch_hole),
      * the prealloc allocation is contiguous (a single
        BtrfsExtentRewriteSegment::Prealloc record, not two
        adjacent ones — the regression coalesce helper from
        e166fbb is exactly what this test pins),
      * subsequent reads return zeros.

Whitespace touch on btrfs_push_prealloc_rewrite_segment lets
checked_add chain across multiple lines for readability; no
behavior change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
